title: The Raven
author: Edgar Allan Poe
content: “Deep into that darkness peering, long I stood there wondering, fearing.”
action: Face the uncertainty you are avoiding.

title: Sonnet 18
author: William Shakespeare
content: “So long as men can breathe or eyes can see, So long lives this, and this gives life to thee.”
action: Produce work that survives your own time.

title: Dover Beach
author: Matthew Arnold
content: “And we are here as on a darkling plain... where ignorant armies clash by night.”
action: Define your position in a chaotic environment.

title: The Tyger
author: William Blake
content: “What immortal hand or eye, Could frame thy fearful symmetry?”
action: Analyze the structure of your greatest challenge.

title: Ode on a Grecian Urn
author: John Keats
content: “Beauty is truth, truth beauty,—that is all Ye know on earth, and all ye need to know.”
action: Strip your project down to the essential truth.
